decision_taken: lot_number is carried and read back through the contract surface (getHarvestLog), but NO glossary term can set or assert it, so it is oracle-unverifiable; already flagged in scope.md as elicitation-menu material shared with seeding/input
reversal_condition: a lot_number glossary term lands (auto-promotes: 3 features share it)

decision_taken: movement_recorded reuses the 9h5.24 kind verbatim (isLog:false per the bound taxonomy decision); the other three are feature-registered kinds proposed for wave-boundary freezing
reversal_condition: wave-boundary kernel freeze renames or merges them

decision_taken: generated name = season labels joined '/' + ' ' + crop labels joined ', ' (contract wording); the location component (transplant preferred over seeding) informs location, not the name string, because the contract's format sentence names only seasons and crops
reversal_condition: an observed display-name fixture showing the location in the generated name

decision_taken: the contract type is {yieldTotal, logCount}; the frozen PENDING_PARTNER pairing requires the excluded mass to be visible, so the projection returns pendingYieldTotal/pendingLogCount ALONGSIDE (a superset of the contract type, never blended into the officials)
reversal_condition: a reader rules the superset a contract violation rather than the partner obligation

decision_taken: getHarvestTotals folds the (measure,unit) pair of the plant's first quantity-bearing harvest log; cross-pair totals are never summed (per-pair independence)
reversal_condition: a fixture with heterogeneous pairs asserting a specific pair for the total

decision_taken: recordBirth uses the kernel demoteToObservation mechanic verbatim (earliest-hlc-wins, loser demoted, never dropped) — the bound birth-uniqueness rule, though the source REFUSES a second birth at write time (unobservable, per the decision registry's own note)
reversal_condition: Duke re-binds birth-uniqueness to refuse-at-write for the port
